chief_complaint: A 46-year-old man presented with a community-acquired urinary tract infection (CA-UTI) that rapidly progressed to bilateral sensorineural deafness and meningitis.
history: The patient had previously undiagnosed diabetes mellitus. The CA-UTI rapidly progressed to bilateral sensorineural deafness and meningitis. Metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) identified K. pneumoniae harboring hypervirulence genes rmpA and iucA.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Blood, urine, and CSF cultures yielded an ESBL-negative, pansensitive hvKP strain. Brain MRI revealed septic emboli, consistent with hematogenous dissemination.
correct_diagnosis: urinary tract infection (CA-UTI) that rapidly progressed to bilateral sensorineural deafness and meningitis caused by hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae
differentials: urinary tract infection (CA-UTI) that rapidly progressed to bilateral sensorineural deafness and meningitis caused by hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae
correct_ranking: urinary tract infection (CA-UTI) that rapidly progressed to bilateral sensorineural deafness and meningitis caused by hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae
